questions.text: A 65-year-old presents with inferior ST elevation. What is the management?
questions.reference_answer: Activate STEMI protocol. Aspirin 325mg, P2Y12 inhibitor. Primary PCI within 90 min.
questions.domain_summary: STEMI management, 65-year-old, inferior ST elevation
questions.domain_example: A 65-year-old presents with inferior ST elevation. What is the management?

questions.text: What are the indications for primary PCI in STEMI?
questions.reference_answer: STEMI within 12 hours, cardiogenic shock, failed thrombolysis.
questions.domain_summary: STEMI management, 65-year-old, inferior ST elevation
questions.domain_example: A 65-year-old presents with inferior ST elevation. What is the management?

questions.text: How do you manage acute atrial fibrillation with rapid ventricular response?
questions.reference_answer: Rate control with beta-blocker or calcium channel blocker. Anticoagulation if >48h.
questions.domain_summary: STEMI management, 65-year-old, inferior ST elevation
questions.domain_example: A 65-year-old presents with inferior ST elevation. What is the management?

questions.text: What troponin levels indicate myocardial injury?
questions.reference_answer: High-sensitivity troponin above 99th percentile URL indicates myocardial injury.
questions.domain_summary: STEMI management, 65-year-old, inferior ST elevation
questions.domain_example: A 65-year-old presents with inferior ST elevation. What is the management?

questions.text: When is thrombolysis preferred over PCI in STEMI?
questions.reference_answer: When PCI-capable facility cannot be reached within 120 minutes of first medical contact.
questions.domain_summary: STEMI management, 65-year-old, inferior ST elevation
questions.domain_example: A 65-year-old presents with inferior ST elevation. What is the management?

questions.text: What are the contraindications for metformin use in patients with renal impairment?
questions.reference_answer: eGFR <30 mL/min/1.73m2 is contraindicated. Use with caution if eGFR 30-45.
questions.domain_summary: Metformin contraindications and renal safety criteria
questions.domain_example: What are the contraindications for metformin use in patients with renal impairment?

questions.text: What are the major drug interactions with warfarin?
questions.reference_answer: Amiodarone, fluconazole, NSAIDs, broad-spectrum antibiotics increase INR.
questions.domain_summary: Metformin contraindications and renal safety criteria
questions.domain_example: What are the contraindications for metformin use in patients with renal impairment?

questions.text: What class of antibiotics is avoided in children under 8 due to teeth discoloration?
questions.reference_answer: Tetracyclines are contraindicated in children under 8 years of age.
questions.domain_summary: Metformin contraindications and renal safety criteria
questions.domain_example: What are the contraindications for metformin use in patients with renal impairment?

questions.text: How does renal impairment affect aminoglycoside dosing?
questions.reference_answer: Extend dosing intervals and monitor trough levels closely.
questions.domain_summary: Metformin contraindications and renal safety criteria
questions.domain_example: What are the contraindications for metformin use in patients with renal impairment?

questions.text: What is serotonin syndrome and which drug combinations cause it?
questions.reference_answer: Life-threatening excess serotonin. Caused by SSRIs + MAOIs, linezolid, tramadol.
questions.domain_summary: Metformin contraindications and renal safety criteria
questions.domain_example: What are the contraindications for metformin use in patients with renal impairment?

questions.text: A patient presents with a thunderclap headache. How do you rule out SAH?
questions.reference_answer: Non-contrast CT head first. If negative within 6h, LP for xanthochromia.
questions.domain_summary: Thunderclap headache workup, SAH rule-out protocol
questions.domain_example: A patient presents with a thunderclap headache. How do you rule out subarachnoid hemorrhage?

questions.text: What is the initial management of status epilepticus?
questions.reference_answer: Lorazepam IV first-line. Fosphenytoin if benzodiazepine fails. Protect airway.
questions.domain_summary: Thunderclap headache workup, SAH rule-out protocol
questions.domain_example: A patient presents with a thunderclap headache. How do you rule out subarachnoid hemorrhage?

questions.text: How do you differentiate ischemic stroke from hemorrhagic stroke?
questions.reference_answer: Non-contrast CT head. Hemorrhagic shows hyperdensity. Ischemic may be normal early.
questions.domain_summary: Thunderclap headache workup, SAH rule-out protocol
questions.domain_example: A patient presents with a thunderclap headache. How do you rule out subarachnoid hemorrhage?

questions.text: What are the diagnostic criteria for multiple sclerosis?
questions.reference_answer: McDonald criteria: dissemination in space and time on MRI or clinical presentation.
questions.domain_summary: Thunderclap headache workup, SAH rule-out protocol
questions.domain_example: A patient presents with a thunderclap headache. How do you rule out subarachnoid hemorrhage?

questions.text: When is lumbar puncture indicated in bacterial meningitis workup?
questions.reference_answer: After CT rules out raised ICP. Blood cultures first to avoid treatment delay.
questions.domain_summary: Thunderclap headache workup, SAH rule-out protocol
questions.domain_example: A patient presents with a thunderclap headache. How do you rule out subarachnoid hemorrhage?

questions.text: What vaccines are recommended at the 2-month well-child visit per CDC ACIP?
questions.reference_answer: DTaP, Hib, IPV, PCV15/20, RV, HepB (2nd dose).
questions.domain_summary: 2-month vaccination schedule, US CDC ACIP guidelines
questions.domain_example: What vaccines are recommended at the 2-month well-child visit per CDC ACIP guidelines?

questions.text: What are the signs of Kawasaki disease in a 3-year-old?
questions.reference_answer: Fever >5 days plus 4 of 5: rash, conjunctivitis, mouth changes, hand changes, lymphadenopathy.
questions.domain_summary: 2-month vaccination schedule, US CDC ACIP guidelines
questions.domain_example: What vaccines are recommended at the 2-month well-child visit per CDC ACIP guidelines?

questions.text: How do you manage febrile seizures in a 2-year-old?
questions.reference_answer: Most are simple and self-limiting. Antipyretics, reassure parents. Recurrence risk ~30%.
questions.domain_summary: 2-month vaccination schedule, US CDC ACIP guidelines
questions.domain_example: What vaccines are recommended at the 2-month well-child visit per CDC ACIP guidelines?

questions.text: What is the dosing of paracetamol in a 15 kg child?
questions.reference_answer: 10-15 mg/kg per dose every 4-6 hours. Max 75 mg/kg/day.
questions.domain_summary: 2-month vaccination schedule, US CDC ACIP guidelines
questions.domain_example: What vaccines are recommended at the 2-month well-child visit per CDC ACIP guidelines?

questions.text: When is RSV prophylaxis with palivizumab indicated?
questions.reference_answer: Premature infants <29 weeks, infants with CHD or CLD in first RSV season.
questions.domain_summary: 2-month vaccination schedule, US CDC ACIP guidelines
questions.domain_example: What vaccines are recommended at the 2-month well-child visit per CDC ACIP guidelines?

questions.text: What are the steps for BLS in an unresponsive, non-breathing adult?
questions.reference_answer: Call 911, 30 chest compressions : 2 breaths, use AED as soon as available.
questions.domain_summary: BLS protocol, unresponsive non-breathing patient
questions.domain_example: What are the steps for BLS in an unresponsive, non-breathing adult?

questions.text: How do you manage anaphylaxis in the emergency department?
questions.reference_answer: IM epinephrine 0.3-0.5mg in lateral thigh. IV fluids, diphenhydramine, steroids.
questions.domain_summary: BLS protocol, unresponsive non-breathing patient
questions.domain_example: What are the steps for BLS in an unresponsive, non-breathing adult?

questions.text: What is the initial approach to a trauma patient (primary survey)?
questions.reference_answer: ABCDE: Airway, Breathing, Circulation, Disability (neuro), Exposure.
questions.domain_summary: BLS protocol, unresponsive non-breathing patient
questions.domain_example: What are the steps for BLS in an unresponsive, non-breathing adult?

questions.text: How do you manage a tension pneumothorax?
questions.reference_answer: Immediate needle decompression 2nd ICS MCL. Definitive chest tube.
questions.domain_summary: BLS protocol, unresponsive non-breathing patient
questions.domain_example: What are the steps for BLS in an unresponsive, non-breathing adult?

questions.text: What is the Heimlich maneuver and when is it used?
questions.reference_answer: Abdominal thrusts for conscious choking adult. Alternate with back blows.
questions.domain_summary: BLS protocol, unresponsive non-breathing patient
questions.domain_example: What are the steps for BLS in an unresponsive, non-breathing adult?
